iti75:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti75:2.1: “Monks, these three types of persons can be found existing in the world.
iti75:2.3: One like a cloud without rain, one who rains locally, and one who rains everywhere.
iti75:3.1: “And how is a person like a cloud without rain?
iti75:3.2:
There is the case where a person is not a giver of food, drink, clothing, vehicles,
garlands, scents, ointments, beds, dwellings, or lights to anyone at all:
to contemplatives or brahmans, to any of the miserable, the homeless, or beggars.
iti75:3.3: This is how a person is like a cloud without rain.
iti75:4.1: “And how is a person one who rains locally?
iti75:4.2:
There is the case where a person is a giver of food, drink, clothing, vehicles, garlands, scents, ointments, beds,
dwellings, & lights to some contemplatives & brahmans,
to some of the miserable, the homeless, & beggars, but not to others.
iti75:4.3: This is how a person is one who rains locally.
iti75:5.1: “And how is a person one who rains everywhere?
iti75:5.2:
There is the case where a person gives food, drink, clothing, vehicles, garlands, scents, ointments, beds,
dwellings, & lights to all contemplatives & brahmans, to all of the miserable, the homeless, & beggars.
iti75:5.3: This is how a person is one who rains everywhere.
iti75:5.4: “These are the three types of persons who can be found existing in the world.”
iti75:6.2: to the miserable, nor to the homeless
iti75:6.3: does he share what he’s gained:
iti75:6.6: is called a cloud with no rain.
iti75:10.5: he satisfies rightly with food & drink
iti75:10.6: those fallen into the homeless state.
